examTip: WB Geography appears in nearly every exam. Focus on rivers, districts, borders, and state symbols — these are direct mark questions.

sections.heading: 🏳️ State Symbols — Must Know All
rows: Amar Shonar Bangla (by Tagore, also Bangladesh national anthem)

sections.heading: 🌊 Major Rivers of West Bengal
points: Ganges (Ganga) → enters WB at Farakka, splits into Bhagirathi-Hooghly (India) and Padma (Bangladesh)
Hooghly River — main river through Kolkata, part of Ganga system
Damodar River — 'Sorrow of Bengal' (due to floods), now controlled by DVC (Damodar Valley Corporation)
Teesta River — flows through North Bengal, originates in Sikkim, disputed water-sharing with Bangladesh
Mahananda — flows through Malda and North Bengal
Torsa, Jaldhaka, Raidak — North Bengal rivers flowing from Bhutan/Sikkim
Rupnarayan — formed by Shilabati + Dwarkeswar, joins Hooghly at Geonkhali
Subarnarekha — flows through Medinipur, enters Odisha
Kangsabati (Kasai) — major river of Midnapore, Kangsabati reservoir project

sections.heading: 🏔️ Physical Regions
points: Darjeeling Himalaya (North): Sandakphu (3,636m) — highest peak in WB
Terai & Dooars: Foothills zone, tea gardens, forests (North Bengal)
North Bengal Plains: Districts like Koch Bihar, Jalpaiguri, Malda
Rarh Bengal: Old hard-rock plateau region (Bankura, Purulia, Birbhum, Burdwan)
Delta Region: Ganga-Brahmaputra delta — most fertile, Nadia, Murshidabad
Sundarbans: World's largest mangrove forest, UNESCO World Heritage Site, shares with Bangladesh
Coastal Bengal: South 24 Parganas coast, Digha (nearest beach to Kolkata)
Siliguri Corridor (Chicken's Neck): Only 22-33 km wide strip connecting NE India to rest of India

sections.heading: 🏙️ Important Districts to Know
rows: Tea gardens, Tiger Hill, border with Nepal/Bhutan/Sikkim
Koch Kingdom, shares border with Assam & Bhutan
Mango (Malda variety), border with Bihar & Bangladesh
Former Nawab capital, Hazarduari Palace, silk
Tribal area, Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary, coal mines
State capital, largest city, River Hooghly
